questions.q: A company manufactures two products A and B. The profit on the product A is ₹ 10 per unit and that on product B is ₹ 5 per unit. The maximum time available for production of both the products is 120 hours. The manufacturing of product A requires 2 hours, while that of product B requires 3 hours. Formulate the given problem as an LPP and find the maximum profit.
questions.answer: Maximize Z = 10x + 5y, subject to 2x + 3y ≤ 120, x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0

questions.q: The objective function for the following LPP is to be minimised.
The constraints in a linear programming problem are x + y ≤ 8, x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0. The feasible solution set formed by these constraints has a shape of a

A firm makes two products, A and B with the profit of ₹ 10 per unit and ₹ 5 per unit respectively. The maximum time available for the production of both the products is 120 hours. The manufacturing of product A requires 2 hours, while that of product B requires 3 hours. If the firm produces product A for 60 hours, then find the number of hours for which the firm will be idle.
The given problem can be represented by the following LPP.

A company produces two products: a machine part and a car. The production time per unit of machine parts is 2 hours whereas it is 3 hours for a car. The company has 120 hours available each week. If machine parts are sold for ₹ 10 each and cars for ₹ 50 each, then form the problem as a linear programming problem and find the objective function.
questions.answer: Maximize Z = 10x + 50y, subject to 2x + 3y ≤ 120, x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0

The shadow price of the constraint 2x + 3y ≤ 120 is 1.
